text: Chapter 2 contains no load-bearing prose passages — composed entirely of notated diagrams showing Phase II vertical transpositions of the chord inversions and the five basic forms of activity across all twelve keys (Abm7, Am7, Bbm7, Bm7, Cm7, Dbm7, Dm7, Ebm7, Em7, Fm7, Gbm7, Gm7) in a single sector of the fingerboard. The instructional content is delivered purely through fingerboard graphics and notated examples rather than prose; the operative rule (chord inversion dictates the matching line form) was already established in Phase I and is simply applied here vertically rather than horizontally. No new terminology or concepts are introduced in prose form.
